answer.text: A mutual fund scheme launched by SEBI
A centralized digital platform for mutual fund investors to access services like CAS, redemption, and portfolio tracking across all fund houses
The office of AMFI (Association of Mutual Funds in India)
A stock exchange for trading mutual fund units

explanation.text: **The Logic:** MFCentral is a centralized digital platform created jointly by CAMS and KFintech (the two main RTAs). It allows investors to access various mutual fund services in one place, including viewing CAS, tracking portfolio, initiating redemptions, managing bank mandates, and updating KYC. Investors can access it through a website or mobile app. **The Trap:** MFCentral is not a mutual fund scheme or a stock exchange. It is a service platform. It does not directly manage investments but provides a single window to access services across all fund houses. **Cross-Exam Context:** Also tested in V-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** MFCentral is very useful for retired investors who have investments across multiple fund houses. Help them register on MFCentral so they can view their entire portfolio, get CAS, and manage their investments from one platform.
